content.problemStatement:
Chef is given a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.

Chef considers a contiguous subsequence Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_rAl‚Äč,Al+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ar‚Äč (where 1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N1 \le l \le r \le N1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N) fruitful if it satisfies the condition
‚ą£Al‚ąíAr‚ą£=max(Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶,Ar)‚ąímin(Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶,Ar)‚ÄČ.|A_l - A_r| = \mathrm{max}(A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r) - \mathrm{min}(A_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_r) \,.‚ą£Al‚Äč‚ąíAr‚Äč‚ą£=max(Al‚Äč,Al+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ar‚Äč)‚ąímin(Al‚Äč,Al+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ar‚Äč).

Please help Chef find the number of fruitful contiguous subsequences of the sequence AAA.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the number of fruitful contiguous subsequences.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: All contiguous subsequences, i.e. [1][1][1], [2][2][2], [3][3][3], [1,2][1,2][1,2], [2,3][2,3][2,3] and [1,2,3][1,2,3][1,2,3], are fruitful.
